dsl_rule.translation_notes: Signal describes mean reversion using standard deviation thresholds. Mapped to Bollinger Bands (bb_upper, bb_middle, bb_lower) which encode mean (middle) and ±1 stddev (upper/lower) bands at default 20-period lookback. Entry when price crosses below lower band (short) or above upper band (long) with confirmation of movement away from mean (price < prev_close for long, price > prev_close for short). Exit when price reverts to middle band (mean). Hard stop at 2.0 stddev implemented via stop_loss_pct. Hold_days_max set to 10 bars as specified.

backtest_rule.reason: Signal describes mean reversion using standard deviation thresholds. Mapped to Bollinger Bands (bb_upper, bb_middle, bb_lower) which encode mean (middle) and ±1 stddev (upper/lower) bands at default 20-period lookback. Entry when price crosses below lower band (short) or above upper band (long) with confirmation of movement away from mean (price < prev_close for long, price > prev_close for short). Exit when price reverts to middle band (mean). Hard stop at 2.0 stddev implemented via stop_loss_pct. Hold_days_max set to 10 bars as specified.

source_rule_text: Enter a trade when a price series deviates significantly from its mean, expecting reversion to that mean, and exit when the series returns to the mean for profit.
